annotations.label: Chebyshev polynomials of the first kind (trigonometric)

anchor.snippet: The Chebyshev polynomials of the first kind

annotations.note: `T_real_cos` (and its complex counterpart `T_complex_cos`) state `(T ℝ n).eval (cos θ) = cos (n * θ)`, the trigonometric definition.

annotations.label: Chebyshev polynomials of the second kind (trigonometric)

anchor.snippet: Similarly, the Chebyshev polynomials of the second kind

annotations.note: `U_real_cos` and `U_complex_cos` express `(U R n).eval (cos θ) * sin θ = sin ((n + 1) * θ)`.

annotations.label: Extremal property of T_n

anchor.snippet: are polynomials with the largest possible leading coefficient whose absolute value on the interval

annotations.note: `leadingCoeff_le_of_forall_abs_le_one` and `leadingCoeff_eq_iff_of_forall_abs_le_one` characterize `T_n` as the unique polynomial of degree `n` with maximal leading coefficient `2^(n-1)` under the `|P| ≤ 1` constraint on `[-1,1]`.

annotations.label: Recurrence definition of T_n

anchor.snippet: The Chebyshev polynomials of the first kind can be defined by the recurrence relation

annotations.note: `T` is defined by the recurrence `T (n+2) = 2 * X * T (n+1) - T n` with `T_zero = 1`, `T_one = X`.

annotations.label: Recurrence definition of U_n

anchor.snippet: The Chebyshev polynomials of the second kind can be defined by the recurrence relation

annotations.note: `U` is defined by `U (n+2) = 2 * X * U (n+1) - U n` with `U_zero = 1`, `U_one = 2 * X`.

annotations.label: Trigonometric definition (unique polynomials)

anchor.snippet: can be defined as the unique polynomials satisfying

annotations.note: The identity `T_n(cos θ) = cos(n θ)` is proved as `T_complex_cos`/`T_real_cos`, but uniqueness of such polynomials is not packaged as a single statement.

annotations.label: Definition via complex exponentiation

anchor.snippet: An equivalent way to state this is via exponentiation of a complex number

annotations.note: Mathlib relates `T_n` to `cos` and `cosh` but does not state the `(cos θ + i sin θ)^n` form explicitly.

annotations.label: T_n is a polynomial of degree n in x

annotations.note: `degree_T`/`natDegree_T` give `(T R n).natDegree = n.natAbs` over a domain with `NeZero (2 : R)`.

annotations.label: Definition extension outside [-1,1]

anchor.snippet: outside the interval

annotations.note: `T_real_cosh` proves `(T ℝ n).eval (cosh θ) = cosh (n * θ)`, the standard extension for `|x| ≥ 1`.
